findings.issue: Negative-branch plang test for `if X exists` is written as `.test.goal2`, which `plang --test` does not discover. The file is parked. v5 finding F4 stands.
findings.impact: A revert of the F3 fix (where `if X exists` always recurses) is not caught at the plang layer — only C# DefaultEvaluatorTests does. Worse than no file, because the directory listing suggests coverage that does not execute.
findings.suggestion: Rename `.test.goal2` -> `.test.goal`, delete the stale `.build/whenexists.pr`, and rebuild from project root.

findings.issue: Slash-qualified `goal.call` resolution (`GetGoalAsync`) and `LoadFromFile` leaf-match (coder v6 core fix) have zero unit tests. Validated only by self-rebuild smoke.
findings.impact: The four-tier resolution order (caller-ancestor -> context -> root -> leaf-match) can drift on the next refactor with no test going red. The bug coder v6 fixed comes back silently until someone rebuilds the system/builder tree.
findings.suggestion: Add `PLang.Tests/App/Goals/GoalCallResolutionTests.cs` covering: (1) slash name resolved by caller-ancestor walk; (2) slash name resolved root-relative; (3) bare-name regression guard; (4) `LoadFromFile` leaf-match with pre-resolved `prPath` + qualified `Name`.

findings.issue: `builder.actions`'s new `Actions` filter parameter (coder v6) has no test setting `Actions = ...`. The filter path in `Builder.Actions` is unguarded.
findings.impact: Filter could no-op silently (broken case-insensitive match, empty-list semantics flipped). Plang side doesn't notice — LLM gets a larger catalog than asked for, builds still pass because it's a superset.
findings.suggestion: Three cases: Actions=["file.read","file.save"] -> exactly those; Actions=[] -> full catalog (no filter); Actions=["nonexistent.action"] -> empty, no error.

findings.issue: Inverted `File.Exists` fix at line 113 (coder v6) has no regression guard. `AppTests` exercises `Load`/`Save` but never `Builder.@this.RunAsync()`.
findings.impact: Flip `!File.Exists` back to `File.Exists` and the suite stays green; existing apps once again refuse to build without `--app={"create":true}`.
findings.suggestion: Two tests in `AppTests.cs` (or new `BuilderRunAsyncTests.cs`): existing `.build/app.pr` + Create=false -> proceeds; missing marker + Create=false + stdin redirected -> `NoAppFound` ServiceError.

findings.issue: New public `Action.ReturnTypeName` property (typed-returns sweep) has zero test coverage.
findings.impact: This is the LLM's signal for which CLR type a `write to %x%` result will produce — a wrong/null value here cascades into the exact mis-compile classes the builder bot reported. The entire sweep was justified by this signal; the signal isn't tested.
findings.suggestion: Add `DescribeReturnTypeTests`: Task<Data> -> null; Task<Data<bool>> -> "bool"; Task<Data<path>> -> "path"; Task<Data<List<path>>> -> mapped PLang name. Cross-check against `Utility/TypeMapping.cs`.

summary: Test suite is green and stronger than v5 — five of six prior findings honestly closed (F2/F3 mutation-verified red, F1/F5/F6 removed/fixed). One v5 carry-forward (F4: negative-branch plang test parked as `.goal2`, not discovered by the runner). Four new missing-coverage gaps on coder v6 / typed-returns sweep surfaces: GoalCall slash resolution, `builder.actions` Actions filter, inverted `File.Exists`, and `Action.ReturnTypeName`. All structurally fixable with small targeted test additions; none are correctness regressions.
